text: Code refactoring is the process of improving your code's structure without changing how it works. Think of it like cleaning a messy room: you rearrange things to make them more accessible, but you don't throw away anything important. By breaking down large functions into smaller ones, renaming variables clearly, and removing duplicate code, refactoring boosts readability. This makes it easier for other developers—or your future self—to understand and modify the code later. When you skip refactoring, you accumulate "technical debt." This debt slows down development over time because messy code becomes harder to fix and test. Regular refactoring keeps your code clean and maintainable.
